meta.question: What is the origin of SARS-CoV-2 (COVID-19)?

positions.label: Zoonotic spillover at the Huanan market

datasets.label: Huanan market environmental samples & early-case geolocation data

confirmation.note: Audit metadata backfill: preserves the case's prior curator-confirmed decision; the source is a direct supporting source, not a verified dependency quote.

datasets.label: Early SARS-CoV-2 genomic sequences (lineages A and B)

confirmation.note: Audit metadata backfill: preserves the case's prior curator-confirmed decision; the source is a direct supporting source, not a verified dependency quote.

factors.label: Epidemiological proximity of the earliest cases to the market
factors.rationale: Spatial clustering of early cases and positive samples at the market is the load-bearing zoonotic argument.

provenance.quote: the earliest known COVID-19 cases were geographically centered on this market
The Huanan market appears to be a location for amplification, but it is not possible to determine if the first infections occurred there.

factors.label: Ascertainment / reporting bias in early Wuhan case data
factors.rationale: Argues the market centering is not merely a surveillance artifact.

provenance.quote: cases with no known link to the market were nonetheless geographically distributed near it
early case data reflects where testing was concentrated, not where infection began
not fully representative of the viruses in Wuhan early in the epidemic

factors.label: Number of introductions (single seeding vs multiple spillovers)
factors.rationale: Two independent introductions are hard to reconcile with a single lab escape.

provenance.quote: two separate introductions of lineages A and B into humans

factors.label: Furin cleavage site as evidence of laboratory engineering
factors.rationale: Reads the genome features as consistent with natural evolution.

provenance.quote: the acquisition of both the polybasic cleavage site and predicted O-linked glycans argues against manipulation
can be explained by natural evolutionary mechanisms
a furin cleavage site previously unseen in other SARS-like coronaviruses

factors.label: Prior probability of a research-related accident in Wuhan
factors.rationale: Documented intent to engineer a furin site raises the lab prior.

provenance.quote: proposed exactly the kind of engineering that would produce a virus like SARS-CoV-2
collected from the Mojiang mine and passaged in the laboratory
Information and evidence is lacking to assess the possibility of a laboratory origin, so it cannot be ruled out.

factors.label: Reliability of Bayesian priors and likelihood estimates
factors.rationale: The whole conclusion is driven by chosen priors/likelihoods over shared evidence.

provenance.quote: combining the likelihoods across independent lines of evidence
70 to 1 in favor of a lab origin

factors.label: Existence of a close natural bat-virus ancestor
factors.rationale: A near relative in bats supports a natural reservoir.

provenance.quote: 96.2% identical to a bat coronavirus, RaTG13
can bind to ACE2 and use it to enter human cells

sources.title: Worobey et al. — The Huanan market was the early epicenter of the COVID-19 pandemic

position.quote: The Huanan Seafood Wholesale Market in Wuhan was the early epicenter of the COVID-19 pandemic Understanding how severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) emerged in 2019 is critical to preventing future zoonotic outbreaks before they become the next pandemic.

sources.title: Pekar et al. — The molecular epidemiology of multiple zoonotic origins of SARS-CoV-2

position.quote: The first zoonotic transmission likely involved lineage B viruses around 18 November 2019 (23 October to 8 December), and the separate introduction of lineage A likely occurred within weeks of this event.

sources.title: Andersen, Rambaut, Lipkin, Holmes, Garry — The proximal origin of SARS-CoV-2

position.quote: our analyses clearly show that SARS-CoV-2 is not a laboratory construct or a purposefully manipulated virus

sources.title: Crits-Christoph et al. — Genetic tracing of market wildlife and viruses at the epicenter of the pandemic

position.quote: Market-linked SARS-CoV-2 genetic diversity is consistent with market emergence, and wildlife DNA was identified in SARS-CoV-2-positive samples from a wildlife stall.

sources.title: Holmes et al. — The origins of SARS-CoV-2: A critical review

position.quote: there is currently no evidence that SARS-CoV-2 has a laboratory origin

fundingDetails: Biotechnology and Biological Sciences Research Council
Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
European and Developing Countries Clinical Trials Partnership
Ministry of Agriculture of the People's Republic of China

sources.title: Chan & Zhan — The Emergence of the Spike Furin Cleavage Site in SARS-CoV-2

position.quote: Without access to the full set of viral sequences available before the emergence of SARS-CoV-2, it is difficult to know what novel cleavage sites were characterized and how they were introduced.

sources.title: Segreto & Deigin — The genetic structure of SARS-CoV-2 does not rule out a laboratory origin

position.quote: the SARS-CoV-2 chimeric structure and furin cleavage site might be the result of laboratory manipulation

sources.title: Analysis of the DEFUSE proposal (EcoHealth / WIV / DARPA, 2018)

position.quote: the DEFUSE proposal described inserting a furin cleavage site into a SARS-related bat coronavirus

sources.title: Rootclaim (Saar Wilf) — Bayesian analysis of COVID-19 origins (lab leak)

position.quote: our Bayesian analysis concludes a laboratory origin is substantially more likely than a market spillover

sources.title: Weissman — An Inconvenient Probability: Bayesian analysis of COVID origins (lab ~70:1)

position.quote: a likelihood of about 70 to 1 in favor of a lab origin, and probably no less than 28 to 1

sources.title: Peter Miller — The case for zoonosis (COVID origins debate)

position.quote: the epidemiological proximity of the earliest cases to the market outweighs the genetic anomalies

sources.title: WHO-China Joint Study on the origins of SARS-CoV-2

position.quote: introduction through a laboratory incident was assessed to be an extremely unlikely pathway

sources.title: US ODNI — Updated Assessment on COVID-19 Origins
authors: Office of the Director of National Intelligence

position.quote: the IC remains divided; all agencies assess that both a natural and laboratory-associated origin remain plausible

sources.title: Wade — The origin of COVID: Did people or nature open Pandora's box at Wuhan?

position.quote: the furin cleavage site and the lack of an identified intermediate host point toward a laboratory escape

sources.title: Response to Segreto & Deigin — There is still no evidence of SARS-CoV-2 laboratory origin

position.quote: neither the furin cleavage site nor the genome structure constitutes evidence of laboratory manipulation

sources.title: Scott Alexander — I watched 15 hours of COVID origins arguments so you don't have to

position.quote: both judges decided in favor of Peter; I found the evidence in favor of zoonotic origin to be strong though not insurmountable

sources.title: Zhou et al. — A pneumonia outbreak associated with a new coronavirus of probable bat origin (RaTG13)

position.quote: SARS-CoV-2 is 96.2% identical at the whole-genome level to a bat coronavirus, RaTG13

sources.title: Temmam et al. — Bat coronaviruses related to SARS-CoV-2 and infectious for human cells (BANAL-52, Laos)

position.quote: BANAL-52 shares 96.8% of its genome with SARS-CoV-2 and its receptor-binding domain can use human ACE2

sources.title: Boni et al. — Evolutionary origins of the SARS-CoV-2 sarbecovirus lineage (Nature Microbiology)

position.quote: the receptor binding motif of SARS-CoV-2 appears to be an ancestral trait shared with bat viruses, not one acquired recently

sources.title: Lytras et al. — Exploring the natural origins of SARS-CoV-2

position.quote: the features of SARS-CoV-2 are consistent with a natural origin in the sarbecovirus reservoir

sources.title: Garry — SARS-CoV-2 furin cleavage site was not engineered
sources.venue: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences

position.quote: the sequence and structure of the SARS-CoV-2 furin cleavage site are not consistent with laboratory engineering

fundingDetails: HHS | NIH | NIAID | Division of Microbiology and Infectious Diseases, National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases

sources.title: Liu, Gao et al. — Surveillance of SARS-CoV-2 at the Huanan Seafood Market (Chinese CDC)

position.quote: environmental samples positive for SARS-CoV-2 were found in the market, co-located with wildlife stalls

sources.title: Bloom — Recovery of deleted deep sequencing data sheds light on the early Wuhan epidemic

position.quote: the Huanan market sequences that are the focus of the WHO-China report are not fully representative of the viruses in Wuhan early in the epidemic

sources.title: Harrison & Sachs — A call for an independent inquiry into the origin of SARS-CoV-2 (PNAS)
sources.venue: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences

position.quote: the origin of SARS-CoV-2 remains uncertain and a research-related origin has not been ruled out

sources.title: Relman — To stop the next pandemic, we need to unravel the origins of COVID-19 (PNAS)
sources.venue: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences

position.quote: we must remain open to the possibility of a laboratory origin until a thorough, objective investigation is completed

sources.title: Quay — Bayesian analysis of SARS-CoV-2 origin (lab, high probability)

position.quote: a Bayesian analysis estimates the probability of a laboratory origin at greater than 99%

sources.title: Courtier-Orgogozo & de Ribera — SARS-CoV-2 infection at the Wuhan market: a Bayesian analysis

position.quote: the market association is compatible with a human introduction and does not by itself establish a zoonotic origin

sources.title: Latham & Wilson — The Mojiang Miners Passage hypothesis for the origin of SARS-CoV-2

position.quote: SARS-CoV-2 may have originated from a virus collected from the Mojiang mine and passaged in the laboratory

sources.title: WHO SAGO — Independent assessment of the origins of SARS-CoV-2
authors: Scientific Advisory Group for the Origins of Novel Pathogens (SAGO)
sources.population: Global SARS-CoV-2 origins evidence through June 2025

position.quote: The weight of available evidence reviewed by SAGO suggests zoonotic spillover, but significant data gaps remain and SAGO is not in a position to rule out a laboratory origin.

population.label: Global SARS-CoV-2 origins evidence through June 2025
